subject: Re: Crash on subtomogram export
body:
Yep, I'm now splitting the particles by tilt series in order to narrow down 
where the trouble is. Quite tedious clicking through the GUI. We'll see.

On Tuesday, August 1, 2023 at 4:19:56 PM UTC+1 cla...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi James,
>
> The problem most likely lies with a specific tilt series (or particle) and 
> as a quick check you could remove the troublesome tomogram's particles from 
> the star file and try again. I've had a similar issue where my particle 
> coordinates were outside the tomogram (i.e. the star file contained 
> negative numbers). You can look in the subtomo folder at the particles 
> being extracted and see if it stops mid-way during a tomogram extraction 
> (the next particle will be your problem) and check your star file for 
> anything odd related to that particle.
>
> Cheers,
>
> Mat
>
> On Tue, 1 Aug 2023 at 15:22, James Hooker <jho...@mrc-lmb.cam.ac.uk> 
> wrote:
>
>> Hello all,
>>
>> I'm trying to export some subtomograms, but after Warp completes the 
>> boxes for 1-and-a-bit tilt series, it announces that it has crashed. One of 
>> its threads keeps writing out subtomograms for quite a while, but not all 
>> of them, and no star file gets written. Is this something anyone has seen 
>> before?
>>
>> Best,
>> James
>>
>> -- 
>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups 
>> "Warp" group.
>> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send an 
>> email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com.

>> .
>>
>
